diagrams.title: File Attributes Structure
diagrams.description: Shows the typical attributes stored for each file in the file system metadata
diagrams.purpose: Understanding what information the OS maintains about each file

diagrams.title: Sequential vs Direct Access Comparison
diagrams.description: Visual comparison of how records are accessed in sequential and direct access methods
elements: Sequential: Record 1 → Record 2 → Record 3 → ... → Record N
Direct: Jump from anywhere to Record N directly
File position pointer movement shown for both methods
Time complexity comparison (O(1) for direct, O(n) for sequential to reach nth record)
diagrams.purpose: Distinguishing between the two primary file access methods and their performance characteristics

diagrams.title: File Operations Flow Diagram
diagrams.description: Shows the sequence of operations from file creation to closure and the system resources involved
elements: Data flow: User buffer ↔ System buffer ↔ Disk storage
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the lifecycle of file operations and system resource management
